_meta.description: Wave 2 canonical governance policy for exposure normalization, archive isolation, and publication truth.

objective.name: Canonical Resource Exposure And Archive Closure
objective.statement: Every governed control or lifecycle owner must either resolve to a real runtime table/entity or be exposed through a canonical service-backed slice. Legacy aliases that no longer create business value must move into archive isolation instead of remaining active composition elements.

build_questions.question: Canonical owner for this business object is what exact runtime table or service-backed slice, and why is that the only truth source that should remain active?
What gate, KPI, audit trail, or continuous-improvement loop breaks if this resource disappears?
If the object is service-backed, does it have canonical list/detail/create exposure with stable identity and idempotent mutation?
If this alias is no longer used for operations, has it been moved into archive isolation with traceability retained and active composition blocked?
Is a reported gap truly missing runtime, or only a catalog naming mismatch that should be normalized at the source?
Can an auditor trace from the canonical object to every supporting correction, approval, or release record without split truth?
Does every create-side-effect action replay safely under retry without duplicate business records?
If this element has no active function, has it been classified into unused or archive isolation instead of left ambiguous in the live system?
